tournament.name: Vinea Goblin Festival by One Eyed Jack

decks.notes: This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of countermagic, discard spells, and removal. It also utilizes card draw and graveyard synergy to maintain card advantage and generate value. The deck's win condition is to stall the game long enough to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of Zombie tokens created by Zombie Infestation, backed up by evasive threats like Krovikan Horror and Squee, Goblin Nabob. The mana base is flexible enough to support the various color requirements of the spells in the deck.

The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by removing threats with efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate, while also utilizing hand disruption with Duress. The combination of Exalted Angel and Eternal Dragon provides a strong and evasive win condition, while Skeletal Scrying provides card advantage. Tsabo's Web can shut down specific strategies, and Decree of Justice can create a swarm of tokens. Overall, the deck focuses on control elements to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's resources and controlling the board by utilizing land destruction spells like Pillage and Stone Rain. In addition, it utilizes burn spells such as Lightning Bolt and creatures like Grim Lavamancer to deal with threats. The deck also has the ability to search for specific answers with Burning Wish and has recurrent graveyard hate with Phyrexian Furnace. With a land destruction mana base and versatile removal, this Mono Red Control deck aims to lock down the opponent and secure victory through attrition.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by removing threats from the opponent's side of the board through cards like Swords to Plowshares, Vindicate, and Gerrard's Verdict. It also utilizes a combination of card draw and mana denial to stay ahead in the game, with options like Skeletal Scrying and Dust Bowl/Wasteland. The deck also has a strong win condition in Exalted Angel and Decree of Justice, providing a backup plan if the game goes long. Overall, this deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent in a grindy game.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal spells, and efficient card draw. The main win condition is Psychatog, a powerful creature that can quickly grow in size by discarding cards. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Stifle and Chain of Vapor to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Standstill is used to slow down the game and draw cards, while Parallax Tide can help reset the board if needed. Overall, this deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through card advantage and efficient creature threats.

The focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low-cost, high aggression creatures and burn spells. The goal is to deal damage as quickly and efficiently as possible, often winning the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize. Cards like Ball Lightning, Grim Lavamancer, and Lightning Bolt are key components in dealing consistent damage, while Sulfuric Vortex ensures that the pressure remains constant. The deck aims to take advantage of its speed and reach to end the game before the opponent can establish a solid defense.

The primary focus of this deck is to control the board through a combination of counter spells, removal, and evasive threats. By utilizing powerful angels like Lightning Angel and Exalted Angel, along with efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to neutralize threats while setting up its own win condition. The inclusion of cards like Fact or Fiction and Impulse helps to maintain card advantage and ensure the deck can answer any threats presented by the opponent. With a versatile mana base and a mix of control elements, this deck aims to outmaneuver and outlast its opponents in 1 vs 1 matches.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through countering spells, removing threats, and using standstill to further slow down the opponent. The deck utilizes a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to maintain board control. The inclusion of Parallax Tide and Standstill allows for further disruption and card advantage, while cards like Wrath of God and Decree of Justice provide board wipes and token generation. The deck also includes utility lands like Dust Bowl and Faerie Conclave for additional control options.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive spells, focusing on dealing damage quickly and disrupting the opponent's strategy. It uses a mix of aggressive creatures like Ravenous Rats and Hypnotic Specter, along with removal spells like Terminate and Lightning Bolt to control the board. Cabal Therapy and Duress help disrupt the opponent's hand, while cards like Phyrexian Arena provide card advantage. The deck also includes some utility lands like Mishra's Factory to further enhance its aggressive game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures, such as Bladewing the Risen and Rorix Bladewing, and then bring them back into play using spells like Exhume and Reanimate. Dark Ritual helps to accelerate the mana needed for these powerful plays, while cards like Cabal Therapy and Buried Alive provide additional graveyard manipulation. By efficiently utilizing the graveyard as a resource, this Mono Black Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with large creatures and aggressive plays.

The main theme of this deck is to establish a lock on the opponent by utilizing the Stasis enchantment to prevent them from untapping their permanents. This is supported by a mix of counter spells and card draw to maintain control and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also features acceleration from cards like Elvish Spirit Guide and Root Maze to power out the Stasis lock quickly. Black Vise adds additional pressure to the opponent while Forsaken City provides a way to continue paying for Stasis without tapping lands.

This deck focuses on establishing a lock on the opponent by utilizing the card Stasis in combination with other control elements like Counterspell and Chain of Vapor. The strategy is to restrict the opponent's ability to untap their lands, while maintaining card advantage and disruption through cards like Gush and Impulse. By stalling the game with Stasis and protecting it with counterspells, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through incremental damage from Black Vise or by resolving a game-ending spell like Upheaval.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat that can be difficult for opponents to deal with. The deck is designed to support this combo with card draw, counterspells, and manipulation to find the necessary pieces and protect the combo once it is assembled. Additionally, the inclusion of Mishra's Factory provides a backup win condition and additional pressure on the opponent.

The Mono Black Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's hand and board while dealing incremental damage through cards like The Rack and Hypnotic Specter. It utilizes efficient removal spells like Mutilate and Cursed Scroll to control the game. The deck also has disruptive cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Mishra's Factory and Wasteland provide additional utility and a way to pressure the opponent's life total. Overall, the deck aims to grind out the opponent with card advantage and efficient removal spells.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly build up your graveyard with cards that can be cast for free or at a reduced cost using Madness or Threshold mechanics. By efficiently filling your graveyard and utilizing cards like Arrogant Wurm and Roar of the Wurm, you can overwhelm your opponent with powerful creatures early in the game. The deck also includes counter spells and card draw to maintain control and momentum throughout the match.
